question: Read workspace/web_info.json plus your own domain knowledge to answer: How can research on an agent's planning capabilities, as well as an AI's understanding and simulation of the real world--including improvements in visual perception--be systematically approached? Please outline key research directions and trends in this field, referencing relevant academic papers. Generate a structured research report (recommended length <=900 words) that restates the research question verbatim, outlines methodology/assumptions, details current capabilities and technological requirements, and discusses future implications. Reference every factual statement with IEEE-style inline citations ([1], [2], ...) that map to sources in a References section. Output the final report to workspace/ResearchSystem_ChatVsAgent.json.

    "url": "https://www.arxiv.org/abs/2411.14499",

The paper further reviews world model applications in robotics, autonomous driving, scientific discovery, virtual game simulation, GUI agents, and discusses interpretability and trustworthiness.",
    "keyInsights": "World models serve dual functions: building internal representations to understand world mechanics, and predicting future states to simulate and guide decisions. The survey identifies key challenges including long-term temporal consistency, causal reasoning, physical and semantic constraints, generalization, and scalability.   },
  {
    "id": 3,
    "title": "AI Agent 前沿技术调研 (2024–2025)",
    "publisher": "CSDN Blog (Technical Blog Post)",
    "publicationDate": "2025-08-13",
    "url": "https://blog.csdn.net/qq_30054961/article/details/150338543",
    "contentType": "Technical Blog Post",
    "summary": "This technical survey covers frontier AI Agent capabilities as of 2024–2025, focusing on deployable technologies including orchestration, planning and reasoning, tool use and Computer Use, Agentic RAG, memory systems, security and sandboxing, observability, evaluation benchmarks, and runtime engineering.     "keyInsights": "Key research directions include: (1) planning and reasoning with Chain-of-Thought, ReAct, and Monte Carlo Tree Search; (2) tool use and Computer Use for extending agent capabilities; (3) memory architecture for long-term context; (4) safety and sandbox design as foundational concerns; (5) full-chain observability and process-level evaluation for scalable governance. Security and sandboxing are now designed in from the start, and whole-process observability with process-level evaluation has become critical for scaled deployment."
  },
  {
    "id": 4,
    "title": "Genie 2: A Large-Scale Foundation World Model for Autonomous World Generation",
    "publisher": "Google DeepMind",
    "publicationDate": "2024-12-04",
    "url": "https://deepmind.google/research/publications/genie-2/",
    "contentType": "Research Institute Technical Report / Blog Post",
    "summary": "Google DeepMind's Genie 2 is a large-scale foundation world model capable of generating infinitely many action-controllable, playable 3D environments from a single prompt image.     "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.10675",
    "contentType": "Academic Paper (arXiv Preprint)",

    "url": "https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20260213A03HJK00",

    "publicationDate": "2026-04-14",
    "url": "https://blog.csdn.net/DebugLoom/article/details/160112803",
    "contentType": "Technical Blog Post",
    "summary": "This practical guide covers industrial-grade world model architectures for AI agents as of 2024, tracing the evolution from implicit environment modeling in traditional RL to hybrid symbol-neural representations in multimodal agent systems.     "keyInsights": "Three architectural paradigms: (1) End-to-end with implicit world models (e.g., DeepMind Gatport); (2) World-model-based planning with explicit state representation and counterfactual trajectory sampling; (3) Autonomous agent world models deeply coupled with memory and tool-calling layers for cross-task state transfer.   {
    "id": 10,
    "title": "Beijing Unveils World's First Universal Embodied AI Platform 'Huisi Kaiwu'",
    "publisher": "Beijing Innovation Center of Humanoid Robots (Collaborative effort: China's MIIT, Beijing Municipal Government, robotics enterprises and research institutions); AsianFin",
    "publicationDate": "2025-03-14",
    "url": "https://www.163.com/dy/article/JQK8QP2R05118O92.html",
    "contentType": "Research Institute Update / News",
    "summary": "Beijing launched the world's first universal embodied AI platform named 'Huisi Kaiwu,' developed by the Beijing Innovation Center of Humanoid Robots in collaboration with China's MIIT and municipal government. The platform supports multiple robot body types and operates across diverse scenarios, designed to accelerate integration of intelligent technologies into the real world and advance robotics, autonomous driving, and human-machine interaction.",
    "keyInsights": "Embodied AI integrates AI into physical entities (robots) enabling them to perceive, learn, and interact with dynamic environments — similar to human cognition. The platform enables systematic research on: physical world perception and understanding, task planning and execution in novel environments, cross-body type generalization, and real-to-sim-to-real transfer. This institutional effort reflects a growing trend of world-model-enhanced embodied AI platforms being used as research infrastructure for systematic agent development."
  }
]

{
  "title": "Systematic Approaches to Research on Agent Planning Capabilities, Real-World AI Understanding and Simulation, and Visual Perception Improvements",
  "research_question": "How can research on an agent's planning capabilities, as well as an AI's understanding and simulation of the real world--including improvements in visual perception--be systematically approached?",
  "report": {
    "introduction": "This report addresses the systematic approach to research on AI agent planning capabilities, real-world understanding and simulation, and visual perception improvements. As artificial intelligence evolves from passive tools to active agents capable of reasoning, planning, and interacting with physical environments, establishing rigorous methodological frameworks becomes essential for advancing the field.",

    "methodology_and_assumptions": "The research methodology synthesizes insights from recent academic workshops, peer-reviewed surveys, and technical reports from leading institutions including Google DeepMind, Tsinghua University, and the Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence [1][2][5]. Key assumptions underlying this systematic approach include: (1) world models serve as foundational infrastructure for AI agents to understand and simulate real-world dynamics [2]; (2) planning capabilities emerge from the integration of symbolic reasoning with neural network representations [1]; and (3) visual perception improvements are most impactful when coupled with embodied reasoning in physical environments [7]. The field adopts a dual-paradigm framework distinguishing between internal representation learning and future state prediction as complementary approaches to world modeling [2].",

    "current_capabilities": "Current AI systems demonstrate emerging capabilities across several dimensions. In planning and reasoning, methods such as Chain-of-Thought, ReAct, and Monte Carlo Tree Search enable agents to decompose complex tasks and evaluate multiple action sequences [3]. World models have advanced from implicit 2D representations to shallow 3D environments with physics-like consistency, as demonstrated by Google's Genie 2, which generates action-controllable playable environments from single prompt images supporting interaction durations up to one minute at 720p resolution [4]. Visual perception improvements are increasingly evaluated through embodied reasoning metrics. Google's Gemini Robotics models achieve advances in generality (adapting to novel situations), interactivity (human-robot-environment interaction), and dexterity (precise physical manipulation) without task-specific training [7]. A critical finding from extensive validation shows that virtual-world testing with video generation models can accurately predict real-world robot policy performance—across 1,600+ real-world validations, virtual-to-real correlation reached 0.88 [7]. The research community has also developed functional benchmarks such as WorldArena, which evaluates world models on their ability to support physical reasoning and planning beyond visual fidelity [8].",

    "technological_requirements": "Systematic research in this domain requires several enabling technologies. First, unified multimodal architectures integrating perception, reasoning, and action capabilities [5]. Second, robust memory systems enabling long-term context retention and cross-task state transfer [3]. Third, efficient inference techniques including scalable computation for complex planning tasks [1]. Fourth, comprehensive evaluation frameworks assessing both functional and visual dimensions of world models [8]. The deployment of physical AI systems demands specialized infrastructure including simulation platforms, real-to-sim-to-real transfer mechanisms, and safety sandboxing designed from inception [3]. Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics-ER exemplifies the integration of visual understanding with embodied reasoning capabilities, enabling robots to generalize to unseen tasks by understanding spatial and causal relationships [7].",

    "future_implications": "The trajectory of this research field points toward several significant developments. World models are expected to become the next stage of multimodal large language models, bridging digital and physical intelligence [5][6]. Physical AI represents a convergence of information AI (text/image understanding) with real-world interaction capabilities, fundamentally expanding AI application domains [6]. The integration of embodied intelligence with cerebellar-brain-body co-evolution principles promises more sophisticated physical agents [5]. As noted by the Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence, the convergence of unified multimodal systems, embodied intelligence, and AI for science will strengthen AI's ability to perceive, understand, and reason about the world [5]. input.content:
{
  "title": "Systematic Approaches to Research on Agent Planning Capabilities, Real-World AI Understanding and Simulation, and Visual Perception Improvements",
  "research_question": "How can research on an agent's planning capabilities, as well as an AI's understanding and simulation of the real world--including improvements in visual perception--be systematically approached?",
  "report": {
    "introduction": "This report addresses the systematic approach to research on AI agent planning capabilities, real-world understanding and simulation, and visual perception improvements. As artificial intelligence evolves from passive tools to active agents capable of reasoning, planning, and interacting with physical environments, establishing rigorous methodological frameworks becomes essential for advancing the field.",

    "methodology_and_assumptions": "The research methodology synthesizes insights from recent academic workshops, peer-reviewed surveys, and technical reports from leading institutions including Google DeepMind, Tsinghua University, and the Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence [1][2][5]. Key assumptions underlying this systematic approach include: (1) world models serve as foundational infrastructure for AI agents to understand and simulate real-world dynamics [2]; (2) planning capabilities emerge from the integration of symbolic reasoning with neural network representations [1]; and (3) visual perception improvements are most impactful when coupled with embodied reasoning in physical environments [7]. The field adopts a dual-paradigm framework distinguishing between internal representation learning and future state prediction as complementary approaches to world modeling [2].",

    "current_capabilities": "Current AI systems demonstrate emerging capabilities across several dimensions. In planning and reasoning, methods such as Chain-of-Thought, ReAct, and Monte Carlo Tree Search enable agents to decompose complex tasks and evaluate multiple action sequences [3]. World models have advanced from implicit 2D representations to shallow 3D environments with physics-like consistency, as demonstrated by Google's Genie 2, which generates action-controllable playable environments from single prompt images supporting interaction durations up to one minute at 720p resolution [4]. Visual perception improvements are increasingly evaluated through embodied reasoning metrics. Google's Gemini Robotics models achieve advances in generality (adapting to novel situations), interactivity (human-robot-environment interaction), and dexterity (precise physical manipulation) without task-specific training [7]. A critical finding from extensive validation shows that virtual-world testing with video generation models can accurately predict real-world robot policy performance—across 1,600+ real-world validations, virtual-to-real correlation reached 0.88 [7]. The research community has also developed functional benchmarks such as WorldArena, which evaluates world models on their ability to support physical reasoning and planning beyond visual fidelity [8].",

    "technological_requirements": "Systematic research in this domain requires several enabling technologies. First, unified multimodal architectures integrating perception, reasoning, and action capabilities [5]. Second, robust memory systems enabling long-term context retention and cross-task state transfer [3]. Third, efficient inference techniques including scalable computation for complex planning tasks [1]. Fourth, comprehensive evaluation frameworks assessing both functional and visual dimensions of world models [8]. The deployment of physical AI systems demands specialized infrastructure including simulation platforms, real-to-sim-to-real transfer mechanisms, and safety sandboxing designed from inception [3]. Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics-ER exemplifies the integration of visual understanding with embodied reasoning capabilities, enabling robots to generalize to unseen tasks by understanding spatial and causal relationships [7].",

    "future_implications": "The trajectory of this research field points toward several significant developments. World models are expected to become the next stage of multimodal large language models, bridging digital and physical intelligence [5][6]. Physical AI represents a convergence of information AI (text/image understanding) with real-world interaction capabilities, fundamentally expanding AI application domains [6]. The integration of embodied intelligence with cerebellar-brain-body co-evolution principles promises more sophisticated physical agents [5]. As noted by the Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence, the convergence of unified multimodal systems, embodied intelligence, and AI for science will strengthen AI's ability to perceive, understand, and reason about the world [5]. Furthermore, the development of benchmarks like WorldArena reveals critical capability gaps—visually realistic world models often fail in functional embodied tasks—guiding future research priorities toward genuine task functionality rather than superficial visual quality [8]. Multi-agent systems and human-AI collaboration are becoming normalized, with Agentic RAG and Computer Use significantly improving real-world task completion rates [3]. The field is transitioning from the 'Tool Era' to the 'Partner Era,' requiring new frameworks for evaluating and deploying AI systems capable of autonomous planning and real-world interaction [6].",

    "research_directions": "Key research directions identified in this systematic review include: (1) causal reasoning and uncertainty quantification in agent planning systems [1]; (2) hybrid symbol-neural architectures for flexible world representation [9]; (3) long-horizon temporal consistency in predictive world models [2]; (4) multi-resolution spatial encoding for diverse physical environments [9]; (5) zero-shot generalization in novel scenarios without task-specific training [4][7]; (6) whole-process observability with process-level evaluation for scaled governance [3]; and (7) unified benchmarks bridging simulation and physical deployment for embodied AI [8][10]. These directions collectively advance AI from information processing toward genuine understanding and interaction with physical reality."
  },

  "references": [
    "[1] ICLR Workshop Committee, \"ICLR 2025 Workshop on Reasoning and Planning for Large Language Models,\" ICLR, Singapore, Apr. 2025. [Online]. Available: https://workshop-llm-reasoning-planning.github.io/",
    "[2] J. Ding, Y. Zhang, Y. Shang, Y. Zhang et al., \"Understanding World or Predicting Future? A Comprehensive Survey of World Models,\" arXiv:2411.14499, Nov. 2024.",
    "[3] CSDN Blog, \"AI Agent 前沿技术调研 (2024–2025),\" CSDN, Aug. 2025.",
    "[4] Google DeepMind, \"Genie 2: A Large-Scale Foundation World Model for Autonomous World Generation,\" Google DeepMind, Dec. 2024.",
    "[5] Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence, \"2025年人工智能十大技术趋势,\" BAAI, Jan. 2025.",
    "[6] Quantum Bit Think Tank, \"2025年度AI十大趋势报告,\" Quantum Bit, Dec. 2025.",
    "[7] Google DeepMind Robotics Team, \"Gemini Robotics and Gemini Robotics-ER: Generalist Robot Models with Embodied Reasoning,\" arXiv:2512.10675, Dec. 2024.",
    "[8] Tsinghua University et al., \"WorldArena: A Functional Benchmark for Embodied World Models,\" Machine Journey, Feb. 2026.",
    "[9] DebugLoom, \"AI Agent世界模型构建实战手册 (2024最新工业级架构图谱),\" CSDN Blog, Apr. 2026.",
    "[10] Beijing Innovation Center of Humanoid Robots, \"Beijing Unveils World's First Universal Embodied AI Platform 'Huisi Kaiwu',\" AsianFin, Mar. 2025."
  ]
}

In planning and reasoning, methods such as Chain-of-Thought, ReAct, and Monte Carlo Tree Search enable agents to decompose complex tasks and evaluate multiple action sequences [3]. Visual perception improvements are increasingly evaluated through embodied reasoning metrics. Google's Gemini Robotics models achieve advances in generality (adapting to novel situations), interactivity (human-robot-environment interaction), and dexterity (precise physical manipulation) without task-specific training [7]. A critical finding from extensive validation shows that virtual-world testing with video generation models can accurately predict real-world robot policy performance—across 1,600+ real-world validations, virtual-to-real correlation reached 0.88 [7]. Fourth, comprehensive evaluation frameworks assessing both functional and visual dimensions of world models [8]. The deployment of physical AI systems demands specialized infrastructure including simulation platforms, real-to-sim-to-real transfer mechanisms, and safety sandboxing designed from inception [3]. The integration of embodied intelligence with cerebellar-brain-body co-evolution principles promises more sophisticated physical agents [5]. As noted by the Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence, the convergence of unified multimodal systems, embodied intelligence, and AI for science will strengthen AI's ability to perceive, understand, and reason about the world [5].
